items.briefing:
Partnered Health has confirmed a cyber incident affecting patient information across its national clinic network — a reminder that Australian healthcare organisations remain a prime target for attackers. Separately, research shows identity-based attacks (such as stolen passwords and compromised logins) have overtaken software exploits as the leading cause of ransomware incidents, meaning weak or reused credentials are now one of the biggest risks for any organisation, including RTOs.

Akamai has also flagged a sharp rise in automated bot attacks targeting businesses across the Asia-Pacific region, which can be used to steal data or disrupt services.

**Tip:** Check that all staff accounts — especially admin or student management systems — use strong, unique passwords and have multi-factor authentication enabled.

featured.title: Partnered Health confirms cyber incident affecting patient information across national clinic network
featured.quote: Major Australian healthcare provider's patient data breach demonstrates critical vulnerability in essential medical infrastructure.
